assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets do not mention Canada’s Bill C-22 advancing to Royal Assent or becoming law. No direct legislative status update is provided.
No item in today’s news references DHS changes to the H-2A eligible-country list or a new Federal Register designation adding countries.

While there is Cuba-related geopolitical/business coverage, none of the bullets report a nationwide internet disruption in Cuba (let alone confirmation by a major monitoring group) lasting 12+ consecutive hours.
No polling or Marseille municipal-race polling coverage appears in today’s bullets, and nothing indicates an RN/RN-aligned first-place first-round voting intention in that contest.

The vaccine-related story concerns a judge striking down RFK Jr.’s policies, not CDC/ACIP documentation announcing a new/expanded active safety surveillance effort covering 5+ million vaccinated people via linked data.
No Federal Register ‘Names of Persons Who Have Chosen to Expatriate’ list is referenced in today’s news, and there is no evidence about list size (3,500+ names).
Today’s bullets contain no China housing-finance policy announcement, and nothing about a nationwide first-home minimum down payment ratio being set to 15% or lower.
The market coverage mentioned (oil, stocks, dollar, recession risk) does not include any cited CME FedWatch (or equivalent) reading showing a 50%+ probability of a rate hike at the next FOMC meeting.
No FAO Food Price Index release is referenced, and there is no evidence in today’s bullets of a 10%+ year-over-year print in any monthly release.
None of the bullets indicate that WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, iMessage, or Meta Messenger has shipped post-quantum cryptography for end-to-end encryption in a production release.
